claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: causal proof, a repair candidate, a new hidden holdout, temporal or real-device transfer, hardware performance, general route-generation advantage, quantum advantage, BQP separation, solved B4/B8/B10, or new credit
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: a post-holdout channel, route-exposure, and diversity attribution of the R150 Casablanca failure

requirements.label: R150 design, result, and trial-row hashes are bound
three backends, three noise models, two routes, and 32 seeds produce 576 executions
full, gate-only, and readout-only models use identical route and seed schedules
Casablanca full-model attribution reproduces the negative R150 sign
route exposure ledgers cover both routes on all three backends
candidate and denominator diversity ledgers cover all three backends
all 24 revealed R150 rows are used only to bind failure labels
result distinguishes channel attribution from causal proof
hardware, general generation, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false
